title: Use shift-aware specialists for year, testing, and wet/dry regimes
summary: The data mixes seasons, testing, and wet compounds, and official F1 testing guidance says testing lap times are not directly representative.
rationale: The data mixes seasons, testing, and wet compounds, and official F1 testing guidance says testing lap times are not directly representative. Grouped validation and covariate-shift weighting are better aligned with that regime mix than one pooled model.
implementation_hint: Train separate experts for slick dry races, wet-compound laps, and `Pre-Season Testing`, or at minimum by `Year`. Validate with `LeaveOneGroupOut` on `Race_Year` or `Year`, then optionally fit a train-vs-test classifier and use its density-ratio proxy as sample weights for the pooled model or blender.
expected_effect: Improves robustness when leaderboard data over-represents a regime that the pooled model underfits, especially testing or wet rows.
risk: Importance weighting can increase variance; small specialist slices may undertrain without strong shrinkage or fallback blending.
